action.reasoning: Observation: Best result was 54.3 safe_follow_steps (iter 9) with ang=-K*angle. Current predict.py is truncated. Iter 12 with depth obstacle avoidance regressed to 28.08. Analysis: The main bottleneck is maintaining the safe zone (1-2m, facing human within 0.5rad) for 500 steps. The facing threshold is tight (28.6 deg). Need better PD control for both distance and angle tracking, no depth avoidance (avoids human as obstacle), and smoother following behavior. Decision: Write complete predict.py with PD control, submit eval immediately to measure improvement and iteration time.
action.command: cd /workspace && python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('predict.py').read()); print('Syntax OK')" && python train.py
